qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) permits arbitrary code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSTI via Template(text=user_input) leading to code execution through compile()/exec(). The advisory describes path traversal via double-slash URI prefix in TemplateLookup.get_template() exploiting an inconsistency between two slash-stripping implementations. These are different root causes (template content injection vs URI-based path traversal), different vulnerable code paths (Template constructor vs TemplateLookup), and different impacts (code execution vs arbitrary file read).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The single qualifying finding (mako_ssti_code_execution) does not identify the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a path traversal vulnerability in Mako's TemplateLookup.get_template() caused by an inconsistency between two slash-stripping implementations when a URI starts with '//'. The finding describes SSTI code execution via Template(text=user_input), which is a different vulnerability (template content injection vs URI path traversal) in a different code path (Template constructor vs TemplateLookup).
evidence: Advisory root cause: inconsistency between Template.__init__ (strips one /) and TemplateLookup.get_template() (strips all /) enabling path traversal via double-slash URI prefix
Advisory vulnerable code: TemplateLookup.get_template() and Template.__init__ slash-stripping logic
Advisory impact: arbitrary file read via path traversal
mako_ssti_code_execution root cause: Template(text=user_input) compiles and executes arbitrary template content via compile()/exec()
mako_ssti_code_execution does not reference TemplateLookup.get_template(), double-slash URI prefix, or the slash-stripping inconsistency
